description: Execution of arbitrary operating system commands due to improper input validation.

complexity_factors.question: Was the injection blind (no output returned to attacker)?
complexity_factors.reason: Blind command injection leaves fewer traces in web logs because there is no visible output, making it harder for the blue team to confirm exploitation from logs alone.
complexity_factors.source: OWASP A03:2021 — Blind injection variants rated lower detectability than direct output injection

complexity_factors.question: Did the injected command execute at OS or root level?
complexity_factors.reason: System-level execution means the attacker has full server access. Containment requires host isolation not just application-level blocking.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1059 — Command execution at system level maps to high impact, requiring host-level containment per NIST SP 800-61 Section 3.3

complexity_factors.question: Did the attacker establish persistence via the injected command (e.g. cron job, backdoor)?
complexity_factors.reason: Persistence means containment of the initial vector is not enough — the blue team must also find and remove the persistence mechanism.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1053 — Scheduled Task/Job: persistence mechanisms multiply the containment effort required

complexity_factors.question: Was the command obfuscated or encoded to bypass input validation?
complexity_factors.reason: Encoded commands reduce the confidence of signature-based detection tools and require deeper log analysis to confirm.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1027 — Obfuscated Files or Information: reduces detection confidence

ttc_ranges.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Low severity incidents should be contained rapidly with minimal escalation
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Medium severity incidents require analysis before containment
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: High severity incidents involve multiple IR phases before containment is possible

rules.description: Alert must be investigated within 10 minutes of being raised
rules.trigger: No alert_investigation_started event within 600 seconds of alert_raised
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2.4: Initial analysis should begin promptly after detection

rules.description: Incident must be confirmed — command execution must be escalated
rules.trigger: No incident_confirmed event in event log
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2: Incident declaration is a required step in the IR lifecycle

rules.description: Host must be isolated — containment_initiated before max_tolerance
rules.trigger: No containment_initiated event before max_tolerance_min seconds
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: For system-level compromise, host isolation is the primary containment strategy

rules.description: Containment must succeed — host isolated or command execution blocked
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Containment must be confirmed before incident can be closed

scenarios.label: Direct command injection — good response
scenarios.description: Blue team detects abnormal process execution, confirms incident, and isolates the host within acceptable time.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31120 fired — OS command separator detected in web request
Analyst-1 opened alert, began process log review
Analyst-2 confirmed abnormal child process spawned by web server
Host isolated from network, command execution blocked

scenarios.label: Blind command injection with persistence — missed containment
scenarios.description: Blue team detects alert but fails to contain before tolerance window. Persistence mechanism not identified. Auto-fail triggered.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31121 fired — encoded command pattern detected
